question.text: The maximum loss in a short straddle (83 strike, call premium 0.20, put premium 0.15) occurs when:

answer.text: USDINR makes a very large move in either direction from the strike
USDINR closes exactly at the strike price
USDINR moves slightly in one direction

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Short straddle max loss = unlimited (theoretically). Losses increase as USDINR moves further from the strike in EITHER direction. The BEPs are 82.65 and 83.35. **The Trap:** Max PROFIT (not loss) occurs at the strike. Max loss has no theoretical limit. **Cross-Exam Context:** Short straddle risk is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Short straddle sellers must monitor positions closely and may use stop-losses to limit downside.
